# Project Progress

## Current Stage

prover (iteration 099 — single substantive prover lane; iter-098 refactor
landed `alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux_sum_comp` parallel structural lemma.
Iter-099 task: fill that lemma's body AND apply it to close
`cechCofaceMap_pi_smul`'s L695 trailing sorry).

Iter-097 prover landed Step 1 (`alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux` body, L478–L494)
but exhausted six tactic-level routes for Step 2; root cause: the iter-096
structural lemma's single-slot `F` family cannot absorb intermediate `eqToHom`
without re-triggering `Fin (...) → ↑s₀` vs `Fin (n+1) → ↑s₀` index-type
unification outside tactic state. Iter-098 plan-agent escalated to refactor
subagent (slug `alt-sum-pi-smul-aux-sum-comp`): refactor inserted a parallel
lemma at L513–L532 with `sorry` body that **splits the morphism slot** into
a family `G : ι' → ((∏ᶜ Z₁) ⟶ (∏ᶜ Z_int))` plus single `E : (∏ᶜ Z_int) ⟶
(∏ᶜ Z₂)`. At the iter-099 call site, the goal's `eqToHom` lands in `E`'s
independent elaboration slot, bypassing the iter-097 Attempt-5 dead-end.

## Stages
- [x] init
- [x] autoformalize
- [ ] prover
- [ ] polish

## Big-picture context

This project formalizes the Jacobian of a smooth proper geometrically irreducible
curve over a field, following Christian Merten's challenge (`references/challenge.lean`).
The nine declarations in `archon-protected.yaml` are the deliverables.

See `STRATEGY.md` for the forward-looking arc. Phase A's blocker shape:
iter-093 = LinearMap.comp_apply eqToHom-mismatch; iter-094 = HOU on `key₂` /
`Preadditive.sum_comp`-application to nested-`i` summand body; iter-095
exhausted three tactic-level routes; iter-096 refactor added abstract
structural lemma; iter-097 closed Step 1 + exhausted 6 bridging routes for
Step 2; iter-098 refactor split the morphism slot into G+E. Iter-099 closes
both new sorries via the split-slot Miller-pattern application.

## Iter-098 results — independently verified iter-099 plan pass

| File | Sorries | Compiles? | Iter-098 outcome (verified iter-099) |
|---|---:|---|---|
| `Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean` | 7 | ✅ | iter-098 refactor: inserted `alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux_sum_comp` at L513–L532 with `sorry` body (parallel structural lemma; split morphism slot — `G : ι' → ((∏ᶜ Z₁) ⟶ (∏ᶜ Z_int))` family + `E : (∏ᶜ Z_int) ⟶ (∏ᶜ Z₂)` intermediate; `ι_int : Type u` matches universe; per-summand hypothesis on `G i ≫ E` composition). `alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux` (L462–L494) unchanged — iter-097 body preserved. `cechCofaceMap_pi_smul` (L534-onward) unchanged — iter-097 B1 bridge `simp_rw [← ModuleCat.piIsoPi_hom_ker_subtype_apply Z₂ j]` at L656 preserved; L695 (former L657) trailing sorry preserved. Verified via `lean_diagnostic_messages` severity=error returning `[]` and `sorry_analyzer.py` summary 7. Other 6 sorries shifted: L787 (was L749), L1111 (was L1073), L1139 (was L1101), L1329 (was L1291), L1358 (was L1320) — ~38-line insertion shift. |
| `Differentials.lean` | 5 | ✅ | off-limits this iter |
| `Modules/Monoidal.lean` | 1 | ✅ | off-limits (Mathlib gap deferred) |
| `Jacobian.lean` | 1 | ✅ | off-limits |
| `Picard/Functor.lean` | 1 | ✅ | off-limits |
| `AbelJacobi.lean` | 0 | ✅ | DONE |
| `Genus.lean` | 0 | ✅ | DONE |
| **Total syntactic** | **15** | ✅ | iter-097 closed 1 sorry, iter-098 added 1 structural sorry; net unchanged but landscape repositioned for iter-099 split-slot attack on L695. |

Verification (this pass): `sorry_analyzer.py` confirms 15 syntactic sorries;
7 in `BasicOpenCech.lean` at **L532 (NEW), L695 (was L657), L787, L1111, L1139, L1329, L1358**.
`lean_diagnostic_messages` (severity=error) returns `[]`. No new axioms.

## Current Objectives

The dispatcher fans out one prover for the single file listed below.

### 1. `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean` — fill `alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux_sum_comp` body (L532) AND close `cechCofaceMap_pi_smul` L695 trailing sorry via the new lemma

**File status (iter-099 entry).** 7 sorries (L532, L695, L787, L1111, L1139,
L1329, L1358); FILE COMPILES. Foundation repaired iter-092; iter-097 closed
`alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux` body at L478–L494; iter-097 B1 bridge at L656;
iter-098 refactor added `alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux_sum_comp` at L513–L532
(body `sorry`). Note: line numbers may shift by ±2 lines during your edits; the
prover should verify line numbers via `sorry_analyzer.py` and `lean_goal` at
the start of work.

### Step 1 — fill `alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux_sum_comp` body (L532)

The lemma signature at L513–L531 reads:

```lean
theorem alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux_sum_comp
    {k : Type u} [Field k]
    {R : Type*} [Ring R]
    {ι₁ : Type u} {ι_int : Type u} {ι₂ : Type u}
    (Z₁ : ι₁ → ModuleCat.{u} k)
    (Z_int : ι_int → ModuleCat.{u} k)
    (Z₂ : ι₂ → ModuleCat.{u} k)
    [_mZ1 : Module R ((∀ i, Z₁ i))] [_mZ2 : Module R ((∀ j, Z₂ j))]
    {ι' : Type*} (s : Finset ι')
    (G : ι' → ((∏ᶜ Z₁ : ModuleCat.{u} k) ⟶ (∏ᶜ Z_int : ModuleCat.{u} k)))
    (E : (∏ᶜ Z_int : ModuleCat.{u} k) ⟶ (∏ᶜ Z₂ : ModuleCat.{u} k))
    (e₁ : (∏ᶜ Z₁ : ModuleCat.{u} k) ≃ₗ[k] ∀ i, Z₁ i)
    (e₂ : (∏ᶜ Z₂ : ModuleCat.{u} k) ≃ₗ[k] ∀ j, Z₂ j)
    (hG : ∀ i ∈ s, ∀ (r : R) (y : ∀ i, Z₁ i),
      e₂ ((G i ≫ E).hom (e₁.symm (r • y))) =
        r • e₂ ((G i ≫ E).hom (e₁.symm y))) :
    ∀ (r : R) (y : ∀ i, Z₁ i),
      e₂ (((∑ i ∈ s, G i) ≫ E).hom (e₁.symm (r • y))) =
        r • e₂ (((∑ i ∈ s, G i) ≫ E).hom (e₁.symm y))
```

**Proof recipe (~3 lines).** Distribute `Preadditive.sum_comp` then apply
`alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux` on `fun i ↦ G i ≫ E`:

```lean
intro r y
rw [Preadditive.sum_comp s G E]
-- Goal: e₂ ((∑ i ∈ s, G i ≫ E).hom (e₁.symm (r • y))) =
--         r • e₂ ((∑ i ∈ s, G i ≫ E).hom (e₁.symm y))
exact alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux Z₁ Z₂ s (fun i ↦ G i ≫ E) e₁ e₂ hG r y
```

The `Preadditive.sum_comp` lemma is at `Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Preadditive.Basic`
[verified] with type:

```
{C : Type u} [Category C] [Preadditive C] {P Q R : C} {J : Type _}
  (s : Finset J) (f : J → (P ⟶ Q)) (g : Q ⟶ R) :
  (∑ j ∈ s, f j) ≫ g = ∑ j ∈ s, f j ≫ g
```

Both `G` and `E` are LEMMA BINDERS here, so the `rw` fires HOU-free
(Miller-pattern unification). The `exact` then matches Lean's expected type
`e₂ ((∑ i ∈ s, G i ≫ E).hom (e₁.symm (r • y))) = r • e₂ ((∑ i ∈ s, G i ≫ E).hom (e₁.symm y))`
with the conclusion of `alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux` instantiated at
`F := fun i ↦ G i ≫ E` and `hF := hG`.

**Fallbacks if the proof recipe fails at the LSP level:**
- If `rw [Preadditive.sum_comp s G E]` fails because Lean cannot synthesize
  the `Preadditive (ModuleCat.{u} k)` instance: try `rw [Preadditive.sum_comp]`
  without explicit arguments, letting Lean unify against the goal.
- If `exact alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux ...` fails on type-elaboration of
  `(fun i ↦ G i ≫ E)`: try `apply alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux Z₁ Z₂ s
  (fun i ↦ G i ≫ E) e₁ e₂` then `exact hG` then `exact r` then `exact y`.
- If the simpler `rw [Preadditive.sum_comp]; exact alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux
  Z₁ Z₂ s (fun i ↦ G i ≫ E) e₁ e₂ hG r y` doesn't unify: state the
  intermediate goal via `have h_step : (∑ i ∈ s, G i) ≫ E = ∑ i ∈ s, G i ≫ E :=
  Preadditive.sum_comp s G E; rw [h_step]; exact ...`.

### Step 2 — close `cechCofaceMap_pi_smul` L695 via `alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux_sum_comp`

The L695 goal (post-iter-097 B1 bridge) reads approximately:

```
(piIsoPi Z₂).hom.hom (((∑ i, (-1)^↑i • Pi.lift (fun i_1 ↦
    Pi.π Z₁ (i_1 ∘ ⇑(SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom) ≫
    (toModuleKPresheaf C).map (Pi.lift (fun x ↦
      Pi.π (basicOpenCover ↑s₀ ∘ i_1) ((SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom x))).op))
    ≫ eqToHom ⋯).hom (e₁.symm (r • y))) j =
  r • (piIsoPi Z₂).hom.hom ((... same ...).hom (e₁.symm y)) j
```

where the inner alternating sum lives in `(∏ᶜ Z₁) ⟶ (∏ᶜ Z_int)` for some
intermediate `Z_int` (Pi-product indexed over `Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev
n + 1) → ↑s₀`), and `eqToHom : (∏ᶜ Z_int) ⟶ (∏ᶜ Z₂)` bridges via `dif_pos hRel`.

**Recipe (preferred order):**

```lean
-- Verify the goal at this point via lean_goal first.
-- Then apply the structural lemma at family level, bridging to j-eval afterwards.

-- (S2-1) Lift the post-B1 (piIsoPi Z₂).hom.hom (...) j form to family-level
--        e₂ (...) by reverting j (or working at the e₂-level directly via
--        suffices). Specifically:
suffices h_fam :
    e₂ (((∑ i, <Čech summand without eqToHom>) ≫ <the eqToHom>).hom (e₁.symm (r • y))) =
      r • e₂ (((∑ i, <Čech summand without eqToHom>) ≫ <the eqToHom>).hom (e₁.symm y)) by
  -- bridge family-level h_fam to j-eval goal:
  -- both sides of the goal are (piIsoPi Z₂).hom.hom (...) j;
  -- since e₂ = (piIsoPi Z₂).toLinearEquiv, e₂ z j is definitionally (piIsoPi Z₂).hom.hom z j;
  -- congrFun extracts the j-eval; Pi.smul_apply commutes r •.
  have := congrFun h_fam j
  -- this gives: e₂ ((... ).hom (e₁.symm (r • y))) j = (r • e₂ (...)) j
  -- the RHS = r • e₂ (...) j by Pi.smul_apply; the LHS = (piIsoPi Z₂).hom.hom ... j definitionally.
  simpa [Pi.smul_apply] using this

-- (S2-2) Apply the new structural lemma:
refine alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux_sum_comp Z₁ ?_ Z₂ Finset.univ ?_ ?_ e₁ e₂ ?_ r y
-- Four placeholders:
--   ?_  : the intermediate Z_int (a function Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 1) → ↑s₀ → ModuleCat.{u} k)
--   ?_  : the family G (the Čech summand sans eqToHom): fun i ↦ (-1)^↑i • Pi.lift (...)
--   ?_  : the morphism E := the eqToHom
--   ?_  : the per-summand hypothesis hG

-- Lean elaborates the first three from the goal's literal shape via Miller-pattern unification.

-- (S2-3) Discharge ?hG per-summand using iter-090/091 templates:
· intro i _ r' y'
  -- Goal: e₂ (((-1)^↑i • Pi.lift (...) ≫ eqToHom).hom (e₁.symm (r' • y'))) = r' • e₂ (...)
  -- Standard distribution chain:
  simp only [Preadditive.smul_comp, ModuleCat.hom_smul, LinearMap.smul_apply,
             ModuleCat.hom_comp, LinearMap.comp_apply, Pi.smul_apply, map_smul]
  -- After: most of the chain reduces; remaining is the presheafMap_restrict_collapse
  -- step on the per-summand restriction map.
  rw [presheafMap_restrict_collapse]
  rfl  -- or further distribution as needed
```

**Alternative bridging strategy** (if S2-1's `suffices` form doesn't elaborate):

Revert the iter-097 B1 bridge BEFORE applying the new lemma:

```lean
-- At L656, the iter-097 B1 bridge is:
--   simp_rw [← ModuleCat.piIsoPi_hom_ker_subtype_apply Z₂ j]
-- This converted (Pi.π Z₂ j).hom z to (piIsoPi Z₂).hom.hom z j.
-- To revert: forward simp_rw at the post-bridge goal:
simp_rw [ModuleCat.piIsoPi_hom_ker_subtype_apply Z₂ j]
-- Goal returns to: (Pi.π Z₂ j).hom (((∑G) ≫ eqToHom).hom (e₁.symm (r • y))) =
--                  r • (Pi.π Z₂ j).hom (...)

-- Now since (Pi.π Z₂ j).hom z = e₂ z j definitionally (via piIsoPi_hom_ker_subtype_apply)
-- AND e₂ z j = (e₂ z) j (function eval), we can rewrite to the e₂-form:
show e₂ (((∑G_lit) ≫ eqToHom).hom (e₁.symm (r • y))) j =
     r • e₂ (((∑G_lit) ≫ eqToHom).hom (e₁.symm y)) j
-- (use show or change; if show fails, try change)

-- Then S2-1 onwards.
```

**Hard fallbacks if both bridging strategies fail at the LSP level**:
- If `refine` fails on placeholder elaboration: try giving the new lemma fully
  explicit arguments. The signature is large but each binder is named, so
  hand-typing `alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux_sum_comp Z₁ <Z_int> Z₂ Finset.univ
  <G> <E> e₁ e₂ <hG> r y` is feasible — just verify each argument's type with
  `lean_goal` after a partial `have :=` step.
- If `?Z_int` can't be inferred via Miller: hand-instantiate it. The
  intermediate carrier is `fun (i : Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 1) →
  ↑s₀) ↦ ModuleCat.of k (C.left.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (∏ᶜ fun a ↦
  basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) ↑s₀ (i a))))`. This is `Z₁` itself in the
  iter-097 context (look at L524–L527).
- If the per-summand discharge `?hG` proves tricky: assert a `have` block
  giving the per-summand R-linearity explicitly, then pass it as `hG`. Use
  the iter-090/091 templates verbatim.

**Important**: even if the L695 application fails on a sub-step, FILLING THE
L532 BODY IS SUBSTANTIVE PROGRESS by itself. Do not block Step 1 on Step 2's
success.

### Sorry budget (iter-099)

The lemma may be provable (HOU-friendly proof with free variables) but APPLYING it via `rw`/`simp` fails. **Escalation ladder**: (1) `set F : Fin (n+1) → (X ⟶ Y) := fun i => <summand body> with hF`; the `set` substitutes the body with `F i` in the goal, so subsequent `rw`s have no HOU. Type ascription is CRITICAL — for `(-1)^↑i • <body>`, use `(((-1 : ℤ))^(i : ℕ) : ℤ) • <body>` with explicit casts. May need a preceding `change`/`show` to fold let-bound symbols (e.g. `Z₁`) back from their unfolded form. (2) Push the offending wrapping outside via a second `← ModuleCat.hom_comp` to absorb outer `(Pi.π ...)`-style factors, then apply the distribution with an `i`-independent `?g`. (3) `Finset.cons_induction Finset.univ` to peel summands manually — each cons-step has the fresh index as a HYPOTHESIS (not a binder), so HOU vanishes. **iter-095 EXTENSION** (iter-096 outcome): ALL THREE tactic-level routes proved structurally blocked in practice — (G) `set F` fails because `change`/`show`-fold of `Z₁` would need to match an eta-mismatched literal form; (H) `← ModuleCat.hom_comp` / `← CategoryTheory.comp_apply` fail on def-equal-not-syntactic Pi-product / unfolded carrier types; (I) `Finset.cons_induction Finset.univ` fails typeclass synthesis (`Fintype ?m`). When all three fail, the route forward is to **extract an abstract structural lemma whose summand family is a single lemma-level binder `F : ι' → (M ⟶ N)`**: applying that lemma uses first-order Miller unification (`?F := fun i ↦ <summand body>`) — fundamentally distinct from `rw [key₂]`'s discrimination-tree HOU, which fails on the same summand.
226:- **iter-096**: When a refactor agent tries to bake a complex `let φ_i := ...` expression into a lemma's SIGNATURE, and the body of the `let` contains type-level coercions like `Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 1) → ↥s₀` that need to unify with `Fin n → ↥s₀` (the body's actual type post-`dif_pos hRel`), Lean's elaborator will deterministically `whnf` timeout (no tactic state to apply `dif_pos`). **Workaround pattern**: state the helper at the simpler index type and let the call site rewrite to bridge; OR keep the summand body abstract (as a lemma binder rather than a `let`-binding); OR inline the proof entirely instead of factoring through the helper. The iter-096 refactor confirmed: abstract structural lemmas (with the family `F` as a binder) elaborate cleanly; per-summand helpers with literal Čech bodies baked in their signatures do not.
227:- **iter-096**: When iter-095's tactic-level routes (G/H/I) ALL fail with structural reasons documented at the LSP level, the iter-096 escalation pattern is: extract an abstract structural lemma whose conclusion's UNIFICATION against the goal is Miller-friendly. The lemma's body is a separate proof obligation (sorry-filled by next-iter prover), and so is the per-summand discharge at the call site. This trades 1 syntactic sorry for 2 syntactic sorries — but EACH is HOU-free because the family is named, so the prover can fill both routinely. The iter-094 `key₂` body-local helper had the same intent but body-locally; promoting it to top-level moves the application's HOU into first-order Miller unification (the Lean kernel handles it).

## Mathlib leverage names (iter-096–098 extension)

`alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux` (project-local, iter-096 refactor; **body closed
iter-097**) — abstract structural lemma stating a finite sum of R-linear maps
(via LinearEquivs on both sides) is R-linear in the same sense. Signature has
`F : ι' → (M ⟶ N)` and `s : Finset ι'` as lemma binders. Application uses
Miller-pattern unification, sidestepping the discrimination-tree HOU that
defeated iter-094's `rw [key₂]` and iter-095 routes G/H/I.

`alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux_sum_comp` (project-local, iter-098 refactor, body
sorry — to be closed iter-099) — split-slot extension: `G : ι' → ((∏ᶜ Z₁) ⟶
(∏ᶜ Z_int))` family + single `E : (∏ᶜ Z_int) ⟶ (∏ᶜ Z₂)`. At call site the
goal's `eqToHom` lands in `?E`, an independent elaboration slot, sidestepping
the iter-097 Attempt 5 single-slot `F := fun i ↦ G i ≫ eqToHom` index-type
unification that fails outside tactic state. Body is 3 lines:
`Preadditive.sum_comp` + apply `alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux` on `fun i ↦ G i ≫ E`.

`Preadditive.sum_comp` ([verified] — `Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Preadditive.Basic`)
— `(∑ j ∈ s, f j) ≫ g = ∑ j ∈ s, f j ≫ g`. Used inside
`alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux_sum_comp` body.
